beats.text: AgentCI. Regression CI for AI agents.

A one-line prompt edit — keep answers short — ships to a support agent. The new answer reads fine. It even cites a policy. A reviewer skimming five outputs would approve it. But it silently dropped the refund window the policy requires. Fluent, confident, and wrong.

AgentCI catches it. The regression gate goes red — ten cases flipped from pass to fail. Then a Gemini investigator takes over. Not a script — a real reason-act loop. It pulls the baseline and candidate experiments through the Phoenix M C P server, compares them case by case, and names the root cause in plain English: the brevity instruction is suppressing required policy citations.

A separate agent writes the fix. It restores the full policy contract and drops the harmful constraint. Then AgentCI proves it on a held-out split the candidate never saw, scored by an independent judge. Lift is positive, and held-out regressions stay within the noise floor we measured by re-sampling production against itself. The gate goes green.

Nothing auto-merges. A human approves — and approval mints this exact failure as a permanent eval case, and writes the lesson to Quality Memory. Next time a prompt change touches refund policy, the investigator starts with that lesson, and the regression can never silently return. Every caught failure makes the CI stronger.
AgentCI. Gemini and Google A D K, Phoenix tracing and M C P. Regression CI that improves itself — under your oversight.
